normalized_title: City of Melville Local Planning Policy 2.1 - Non-Residential Development
scope_summary: Design policy applying to all non-residential development and the non-residential element of mixed use development in the City of Melville. Sets built form and amenity provisions covering building design and articulation, corner sites, front facades and shopfronts, awnings and weather protection, ground floor active uses, landscaping, visual privacy where development adjoins residential properties, vehicle access, loading and parking, plant screening, waste storage, and site works. Operates in addition to LPS6 and other applicable local planning policies.

key_numeric_standards.unit: % minimum of facade length adjacent to a footpath
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: ground floor level facades of non-residential development
security shutters/roller doors interfacing with the public domain
awnings where a building abuts a footpath
awnings and any future under-awning signage
awnings (not to be sited within 0.5m of a kerb)
at-grade car parking areas containing more than six bays

key_numeric_standards.unit: m minimum within cone of vision (or 1.6m permanent vertical screening)
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: openings to operational rooms with finished floor level raised 0.5m+ above NGL overlooking adjoining residential property

key_numeric_standards.topic: visual privacy setback - unenclosed outdoor spaces
key_numeric_standards.unit: m minimum within cone of vision (or 1.6m permanent vertical screening)
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: balconies, decks, verandahs with finished floor level raised 0.5m+ above NGL overlooking adjoining residential property
non-residential portion of development exceeding this GFA must provide on-site loading/unloading areas
basement or undercroft levels containing a loading or accessible bay

key_numeric_standards.unit: m maximum height within 1.5m x 1.5m of access way / street boundary intersection
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: structures (walls, fencing, services) and vegetation at vehicle access points

cross_references.cited: State Planning Policy 7.3 Residential Design Codes Volume 1
Planning and Development (Local Planning Schemes) Regulations 2015

quality_flags: source numbering error: clause number 3.5 is used twice ('Windows at ground floor level should remain visually permeable' and 'Reflective or heavily tinted glazing'), so subsequent clauses 3.6/3.7 are offset
diagrams 1-5 referenced in text are not captured in the extracted text (images)
cites NCC 2012 for disabled parking, an outdated edition relative to NCC 2022 in the manifest
